user_query: Update mothers teriyaki salmon bowl lunch to a tofu based dish

updates.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 1 > Mother > Day 2 > Lunch > Japanese Teriyaki Salmon Donburi

short_description.from: Pan-seared salmon fillet glazed with homemade teriyaki sauce, served over a bowl of steamed rice with steamed broccoli and pickled ginger.
short_description.to: Pan-seared firm tofu glazed with a homemade teriyaki-style sauce, served over steamed rice with steamed broccoli and pickled ginger for a satisfying plant-based donburi.

nutrition_note.from: Salmon is rich in omega-3 fatty acids supporting heart and brain health; broccoli provides fiber, vitamin C, and sulforaphane; rice offers sustained energy. Teriyaki glaze contains sodium and sugar — make homemade to control amounts. Pickled ginger aids digestion.
nutrition_note.to: Replacing salmon with firm tofu makes this a plant-based protein option while still providing high-quality protein and calcium (especially if using calcium-set tofu). Tofu is lower in saturated fat than salmon and contains no dietary cholesterol; broccoli adds fiber and vitamin C. The teriyaki-style glaze contains sodium and added sugars, so use low-sodium soy sauce and moderate sweetener to keep the dish balanced. Overall, this swaps marine omega-3s for plant-based protein and a lower-saturated-fat profile; consider adding a small portion of flaxseed or walnuts elsewhere in the day for plant-derived omega-3s.

meal_suggestion.from: Pan-sear salmon skin-side down until crispy, then glaze with a mix of soy sauce, mirin, and a touch of sugar. Serve over steamed rice with steamed broccoli and a small mound of pickled ginger. Garnish with sesame seeds and sliced nori.
meal_suggestion.to: Press firm tofu to remove excess moisture, then cut into thick slices or cubes. Lightly coat with cornstarch and pan-sear until golden on both sides. Prepare a teriyaki-style glaze by simmering low-sodium soy sauce, mirin, a small amount of sugar or maple syrup, grated ginger, and minced garlic until slightly thickened. Toss the tofu in the glaze to coat. Serve over steamed rice with steamed broccoli and a small mound of pickled ginger. Garnish with toasted sesame seeds and sliced nori or scallions.

updates.reasoning: Transformed the teriyaki salmon donburi into a tofu-based donburi. All meal fields rewritten to reflect tofu as the main protein and updated preparation, ingredients, and nutrition note.
